words.name: Lion
words.description:
A large, powerful cat native to Africa and Asia.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape.
2. Start the hand at the forehead.
3. Sweep the hand backward over the head (simulating a mane).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'C' handshape. 2. Start the hand at the forehead. 3. Sweep the hand backward over the head (simulating a mane).

words.name: Elephant
words.description:
A very large herbivorous mammal with a trunk and large ears.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'B' or '5' handshape.
2. Start the hand near the nose.
3. Move the hand downward in an arc (simulating a trunk).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'B' or '5' handshape. 2. Start the hand near the nose. 3. Move the hand downward in an arc (simulating a trunk).

words.name: Dog
words.description:
A domesticated carnivore descended from the wolf, commonly kept as a pet.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the '5' handshape.
2. Pat the upper thigh once or twice.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the '5' handshape. 2. Pat the upper thigh once or twice.

words.name: Cat
words.description:
A small domesticated carnivorous mammal kept as a pet.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'F' handshape (thumb and index fingertips touching).
2. Touch the hand to the cheek near the mouth.
3. Move the hand outward once or twice (simulating whiskers).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'F' handshape (thumb and index fingertips touching). 2. Touch the hand to the cheek near the mouth. 3. Move the hand outward once or twice (simulating whiskers).

words.name: Bear
words.description:
A large, heavy mammal with thick fur and short legs.
How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape, fingers curved like claws.
2. Start the hands on opposite sides of the chest.
3. Cross/scratch the hands downwards twice.
words.howToSign: 1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape, fingers curved like claws. 2. Start the hands on opposite sides of the chest. 3. Cross/scratch the hands downwards twice.

words.name: Carabao
words.description:
A domesticated water buffalo native to the Philippines.
How to sign:
1. Sign BUFFALO: Use both hands in the 'Y' handshape.
2. Hold the hands at the sides of the head, thumbs pointing down.
3. Twist the wrists back and forth.
words.howToSign: 1. Sign BUFFALO: Use both hands in the 'Y' handshape. 2. Hold the hands at the sides of the head, thumbs pointing down. 3. Twist the wrists back and forth.

words.name: Zebra
words.description:
An African wild horse with black-and-white stripes.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the '4' handshape (four fingers up).
2. Draw stripes across the non-dominant shoulder or arm.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the '4' handshape (four fingers up). 2. Draw stripes across the non-dominant shoulder or arm.

words.name: Sheep
words.description:
A domesticated animal kept for wool or meat.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'V' handshape (or 'K').
2. Tap the hand on the non-dominant forearm twice (simulating shearing wool).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'V' handshape (or 'K'). 2. Tap the hand on the non-dominant forearm twice (simulating shearing wool).

words.name: Horse
words.description:
A large, solid-hoofed herbivorous mammal domesticated for riding.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'U' or 'H' handshape, fingers bent.
2. Tap the hand on the sides of the forehead twice (simulating ears).
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'U' or 'H' handshape, fingers bent. 2. Tap the hand on the sides of the forehead twice (simulating ears).
